<a id="api-reference-json-querying-api"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://druid.apache.org/docs/latest/api-reference/json-querying-api/ -->

<!-- page_index: 128 -->

# JSON querying API

This topic describes the API endpoints to submit JSON-based [native queries](#querying) to Apache Druid.

In this topic, `http://SERVICE_IP:SERVICE_PORT` is a placeholder for the server address of deployment and the service port. For example, on the quickstart configuration, replace `http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT` with `http://localhost:8888`.

Submits a JSON-based native query. The body of the request is the native query itself.

Druid supports different types of queries for different use cases. All queries require the following properties:

- `queryType`: A string representing the type of query. Druid supports the following native query types: `timeseries`, `topN`, `groupBy`, `timeBoundaries`, `segmentMetadata`, `datasourceMetadata`, `scan`, and `search`.
- `dataSource`: A string or object defining the source of data to query. The most common value is the name of the datasource to query. For more information, see [Datasources](#querying-datasource).

For additional properties based on your query type or use case, see [available native queries](#querying--available-queries).

`POST` `/druid/v2`

- `pretty` (optional)
  - Druid returns the response in a pretty-printed format using indentation and line breaks.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully submitted query</em></p></div><div><p><em>Error thrown due to bad query. Returns a JSON object detailing the error with the following format:</em></p><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"error"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"A well-defined error code."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"errorMessage"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"A message with additional details about the error."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"errorClass"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"Class of exception that caused this error."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"host"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"The host on which the error occurred."</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div><p>For more information on possible error messages, see <a href="#querying--query-execution-failures">query execution failures</a>.</p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows a `topN` query. The query analyzes the `social_media` datasource to return the top five users from the `username` dimension with the highest number of views from the `views` metric.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/v2?pretty=null" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "topN",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimension": "username",</span> </span><span><span>  "threshold": 5,</span> </span><span><span>  "metric": "views",</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "longSum",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "views",</span> </span><span><span>      "fieldName": "views"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": [</span> </span><span><span>    "2022-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"</span> </span><span><span>  ]</span> </span><span><span>}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/v2?pretty=null HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 336</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "topN",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimension": "username",</span> </span><span><span>  "threshold": 5,</span> </span><span><span>  "metric": "views",</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "longSum",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "views",</span> </span><span><span>      "fieldName": "views"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": [</span> </span><span><span>    "2022-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"</span> </span><span><span>  ]</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   {
>       "timestamp": "2023-07-03T18:49:54.848Z",
>       "result": [
>           {
>               "views": 11591218026,
>               "username": "gus"
>           },
>           {
>               "views": 11578638578,
>               "username": "miette"
>           },
>           {
>               "views": 11561618880,
>               "username": "leon"
>           },
>           {
>               "views": 11552609824,
>               "username": "mia"
>           },
>           {
>               "views": 11551537517,
>               "username": "milton"
>           }
>       ]
>   }
> ]
> ```

The following example submits a JSON query of the `groupBy` type to retrieve the `username` with the highest votes to posts ratio from the `social_media` datasource.

In this query:

- The `upvoteSum` aggregation calculates the sum of the `upvotes` for each user.
- The `postCount` aggregation calculates the sum of posts for each user.
- The `upvoteToPostRatio` is a post-aggregation of the `upvoteSum` and the `postCount`, divided to calculate the ratio.
- The result is sorted based on the `upvoteToPostRatio` in descending order.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/v2" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "groupBy",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimensions": ["username"],</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    { "type": "doubleSum", "name": "upvoteSum", "fieldName": "upvotes" },</span> </span><span><span>    { "type": "count", "name": "postCount", "fieldName": "post_title" }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "postAggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "arithmetic",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "upvoteToPostRatio",</span> </span><span><span>      "fn": "/",</span> </span><span><span>      "fields": [</span> </span><span><span>        { "type": "fieldAccess", "name": "upvoteSum", "fieldName": "upvoteSum" },</span> </span><span><span>        { "type": "fieldAccess", "name": "postCount", "fieldName": "postCount" }</span> </span><span><span>      ]</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": ["2022-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"],</span> </span><span><span>  "limitSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>    "type": "default",</span> </span><span><span>    "limit": 1,</span> </span><span><span>    "columns": [</span> </span><span><span>      { "dimension": "upvoteToPostRatio", "direction": "descending" }</span> </span><span><span>    ]</span> </span><span><span>  }</span> </span><span><span>}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/v2?pretty=null HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 817</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "groupBy",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimensions": ["username"],</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    { "type": "doubleSum", "name": "upvoteSum", "fieldName": "upvotes" },</span> </span><span><span>    { "type": "count", "name": "postCount", "fieldName": "post_title" }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "postAggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "arithmetic",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "upvoteToPostRatio",</span> </span><span><span>      "fn": "/",</span> </span><span><span>      "fields": [</span> </span><span><span>        { "type": "fieldAccess", "name": "upvoteSum", "fieldName": "upvoteSum" },</span> </span><span><span>        { "type": "fieldAccess", "name": "postCount", "fieldName": "postCount" }</span> </span><span><span>      ]</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": ["2022-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"],</span> </span><span><span>  "limitSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>    "type": "default",</span> </span><span><span>    "limit": 1,</span> </span><span><span>    "columns": [</span> </span><span><span>      { "dimension": "upvoteToPostRatio", "direction": "descending" }</span> </span><span><span>    ]</span> </span><span><span>  }</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>     {
>         "version": "v1",
>         "timestamp": "2022-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
>         "event": {
>             "upvoteSum": 8.0419541E7,
>             "upvoteToPostRatio": 69.53014661762697,
>             "postCount": 1156614,
>             "username": "miette"
>         }
>     }
> ]
> ```

Retrieves an array that contains objects with segment information, including the server locations associated with the query provided in the request body.

`POST` `/druid/v2/candidates`

- `pretty` (optional)
  - Druid returns the response in a pretty-printed format using indentation and line breaks.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved segment information</em></p></div><div><p><em>Error thrown due to bad query. Returns a JSON object detailing the error with the following format:</em></p><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"error"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"A well-defined error code."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"errorMessage"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"A message with additional details about the error."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"errorClass"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"Class of exception that caused this error."</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"host"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"The host on which the error occurred."</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div><p>For more information on possible error messages, see <a href="#querying--query-execution-failures">query execution failures</a>.</p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/v2/candidates" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "topN",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimension": "username",</span> </span><span><span>  "threshold": 5,</span> </span><span><span>  "metric": "views",</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "longSum",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "views",</span> </span><span><span>      "fieldName": "views"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": [</span> </span><span><span>    "2022-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"</span> </span><span><span>  ]</span> </span><span><span>}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/v2/candidates HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 336</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>  "queryType": "topN",</span> </span><span><span>  "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>  "dimension": "username",</span> </span><span><span>  "threshold": 5,</span> </span><span><span>  "metric": "views",</span> </span><span><span>  "granularity": "all",</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>  "aggregations": [</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>      "type": "longSum",</span> </span><span><span>      "name": "views",</span> </span><span><span>      "fieldName": "views"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  ],</span> </span><span><span>  "intervals": [</span> </span><span><span>    "2020-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-01-01T00:00:00.000"</span> </span><span><span>  ]</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**

>   {
>       "interval": "2023-07-03T18:00:00.000Z/2023-07-03T19:00:00.000Z",

<a id="api-reference-tasks-api"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://druid.apache.org/docs/latest/api-reference/tasks-api/ -->

<!-- page_index: 129 -->

# Tasks API

> [!NOTE]
> **info**
> This API is not supported anymore and always returns a 404 response.
> Use the metric `segment/added/bytes` instead to identify the segment IDs committed by a task.

---

<a id="api-reference-supervisor-api"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://druid.apache.org/docs/latest/api-reference/supervisor-api/ -->

<!-- page_index: 130 -->

# Supervisor API

This topic describes the API endpoints to manage and monitor supervisors for Apache Druid.
The topic uses the Apache Kafka term offset to refer to the identifier for records in a partition. If you are using Amazon Kinesis, the equivalent is sequence number.

In this topic, `http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT` is a placeholder for your Router service address and port. Replace it with the information for your deployment. For example, use `http://localhost:8888` for quickstart deployments.

The following table lists the properties of a supervisor object:

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | String | Unique identifier. |
| `state` | String | Generic state of the supervisor. Available states:`UNHEALTHY_SUPERVISOR`, `UNHEALTHY_TASKS`, `PENDING`, `RUNNING`, `SUSPENDED`, `STOPPING`. See [Supervisor reference](#ingestion-supervisor--status-report) for more information. |
| `detailedState` | String | Detailed state of the supervisor. This property contains a more descriptive, implementation-specific state that may provide more insight into the supervisor's activities than the `state` property. See [Apache Kafka ingestion](#ingestion-kafka-ingestion) and [Amazon Kinesis ingestion](#ingestion-kinesis-ingestion) for supervisor-specific states. |
| `healthy` | Boolean | Supervisor health indicator. |
| `spec` | Object | Container object for the supervisor configuration. |
| `suspended` | Boolean | Indicates whether the supervisor is in a suspended state. |

Returns an array of strings representing the names of active supervisors. If there are no active supervisors, it returns an empty array.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved array of active supervisor IDs</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   "wikipedia_stream",
>   "social_media"
> ]
> ```

Retrieves an array of active supervisor objects. If there are no active supervisors, it returns an empty array. For reference on the supervisor object properties, see the preceding [table](#api-reference-supervisor-api--supervisor-information).

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?full`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor objects</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?full=null"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?full=null HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   {
>       "id": "wikipedia_stream",
>       "state": "RUNNING",
>       "detailedState": "CONNECTING_TO_STREAM",
>       "healthy": true,
>       "spec": {
>           "type": "kafka",
>           "spec": {
>               "dataSchema": {
>                   "dataSource": "wikipedia_stream",

>                       "column": "__time",

>                       "missingValue": null
>                   },

>                           {
>                               "type": "string",

>                           },
>                           {

Retrieves an array of objects representing active supervisors and their current state. If there are no active supervisors, it returns an empty array. For reference on the supervisor object properties, see the preceding [table](#api-reference-supervisor-api--supervisor-information).

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?state=true`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor state objects</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?state=true"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?state=true HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   {
>       "id": "wikipedia_stream",
>       "state": "UNHEALTHY_SUPERVISOR",
>       "detailedState": "UNABLE_TO_CONNECT_TO_STREAM",
>       "healthy": false,
>       "suspended": false
>   },
>   {
>       "id": "social_media",
>       "state": "RUNNING",
>       "detailedState": "RUNNING",
>       "healthy": true,
>       "suspended": false
>   }
> ]
> ```

Retrieves the specification for a single supervisor. The returned specification includes the `dataSchema`, `ioConfig`, and `tuningConfig` objects.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor spec</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to retrieve the specification of a supervisor with the name `wikipedia_stream`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

Retrieves the current status report for a single supervisor. The report contains the state of the supervisor tasks and an array of recently thrown exceptions.

For additional information about the status report, see [Supervisor reference](#ingestion-supervisor--status-report).

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/status`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor status</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to retrieve the status of a supervisor with the name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/status"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/status HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>     "id": "social_media",
>     "generationTime": "2023-07-05T23:24:43.934Z",
>     "payload": {
>         "dataSource": "social_media",
>         "stream": "social_media",
>         "partitions": 1,
>         "replicas": 1,
>         "durationSeconds": 3600,
>         "activeTasks": [
>             {
>                 "id": "index_kafka_social_media_ab72ae4127c591c_flcbhdlh",
>                 "startingOffsets": {
>                     "0": 3176381
>                 },
>                 "startTime": "2023-07-05T23:21:39.321Z",
>                 "remainingSeconds": 3415,
>                 "type": "ACTIVE",
>                 "currentOffsets": {
>                     "0": 3296632
>                 },
>                 "lag": {
>                     "0": 3
>                 }
>             }
>         ],
>         "publishingTasks": [],
>         "latestOffsets": {
>             "0": 3296635
>         },
>         "minimumLag": {
>             "0": 3
>         },
>         "aggregateLag": 3,
>         "offsetsLastUpdated": "2023-07-05T23:24:30.212Z",
>         "suspended": false,
>         "healthy": true,
>         "state": "RUNNING",
>         "detailedState": "RUNNING",
>         "recentErrors": []
>     }
> }
> ```

Retrieves the current health report for a single supervisor. The health of a supervisor is determined by the supervisor's `state` (as returned by the `/status` endpoint) and the `druid.supervisor.*` Overlord configuration thresholds.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/health`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li><li>503 SERVICE UNAVAILABLE</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Supervisor is healthy</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div><div><p><em>Supervisor is unhealthy</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to retrieve the health report for a supervisor with the name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/health"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/health HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "healthy": false
> }
> ```

Returns a snapshot of the current ingestion row counters for each task being managed by the supervisor, along with moving averages for the row counters. See [Row stats](#ingestion-tasks--row-stats) for more information.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/stats`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor stats</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to retrieve the current ingestion row counters for a supervisor with the name `custom_data`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/custom_data/stats"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/custom_data/stats HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "0": {
>       "index_kafka_custom_data_881d621078f6b7c_ccplchbi": {
>           "movingAverages": {
>               "buildSegments": {
>                   "5m": {
>                       "processed": 53.401225142603316,
>                       "processedBytes": 5226.400757148808,
>                       "unparseable": 0.0,
>                       "thrownAway": 0.0,
>                       "processedWithError": 0.0
>                   },
>                   "15m": {
>                       "processed": 56.92994990102502,
>                       "processedBytes": 5571.772059828217,
>                       "unparseable": 0.0,
>                       "thrownAway": 0.0,
>                       "processedWithError": 0.0
>                   },
>                   "1m": {
>                       "processed": 37.134921285556636,
>                       "processedBytes": 3634.2766230628677,
>                       "unparseable": 0.0,
>                       "thrownAway": 0.0,
>                       "processedWithError": 0.0
>                   }
>               }
>           },
>           "totals": {
>               "buildSegments": {
>                   "processed": 665,
>                   "processedBytes": 65079,
>                   "processedWithError": 0,
>                   "thrownAway": 0,
>                   "unparseable": 0
>                   }
>               }
>           }
>       }
>   }
> ```

An audit history provides a comprehensive log of events, including supervisor configuration, creation, suspension, and modification history.

Retrieves an audit history of specs for all supervisors.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/history`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved audit history</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/history"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/history HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

Retrieves an audit history of specs for a single supervisor.

`GET` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/history`

- `count` (optional)
  - Type: Integer
  - Limit the number of results to the last `n` entries. Must be greater than 0 if specified.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved supervisor audit history</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following examples show how to retrieve the audit history of a supervisor with the name `wikipedia_stream`.

**Get all history entries:**

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream/history"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream/history HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

**Get last 10 history entries:**

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream/history?count=10"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/wikipedia_stream/history?count=10 HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

>               },
>               "replicas": 1,
>               "taskCount": 1,
>               "taskDuration": "PT3600S",
>               "consumerProperties": {
>                   "bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9042"
>               },
>               "autoScalerConfig": null,
>               "pollTimeout": 100,
>               "startDelay": "PT5S",
>               "period": "PT30S",
>               "useEarliestOffset": true,
>               "completionTimeout": "PT1800S",
>               "lateMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>               "earlyMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>               "lateMessageRejectionStartDateTime": null,
>               "configOverrides": null,
>               "idleConfig": null,
>               "stream": "social_media",
>               "useEarliestSequenceNumber": true
>           },
>           "context": null,
>           "suspended": false
>       },
>       "version": "2023-07-05T20:59:16.872Z"
>   }
> ]
> ```

Creates a new supervisor spec or updates an existing one with new configuration and schema information. When updating a supervisor spec, the datasource must remain the same as the previous supervisor.

You can define a supervisor spec for [Apache Kafka](#ingestion-kafka-ingestion) or [Amazon Kinesis](#ingestion-kinesis-ingestion) streaming ingestion methods.

The following table lists the properties of a supervisor spec:

| Property | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | The supervisor type. One of`kafka` or `kinesis`. | Yes |
| `spec` | Object | The container object for the supervisor configuration. | Yes |
| `ioConfig` | Object | The I/O configuration object to define the connection and I/O-related settings for the supervisor and indexing task. | Yes |
| `dataSchema` | Object | The schema for the indexing task to use during ingestion. See [`dataSchema`](#ingestion-ingestion-spec--dataschema) for more information. | Yes |
| `tuningConfig` | Object | The tuning configuration object to define performance-related settings for the supervisor and indexing tasks. | No |

When you call this endpoint on an existing supervisor, the running supervisor signals its tasks to stop reading and begin publishing, exiting itself. Druid then uses the provided configuration from the request body to create a new supervisor. Druid submits a new schema while retaining existing publishing tasks and starts new tasks at the previous task offsets.
This way, you can apply configuration changes without a pause in ingestion.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>415 UNSUPPORTED MEDIA TYPE</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully created a new supervisor or updated an existing supervisor</em></p></div><div><p><em>Request body content type is not in JSON format</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example uses JSON input format to create a supervisor spec for Kafka with a `social_media` datasource and `social_media` topic.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '{</span> </span><span><span>    "type": "kafka",</span> </span><span><span>    "spec": {</span> </span><span><span>        "ioConfig": {</span> </span><span><span>            "type": "kafka",</span> </span><span><span>            "consumerProperties": {</span> </span><span><span>                "bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9094"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "topic": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>            "inputFormat": {</span> </span><span><span>                "type": "json"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "useEarliestOffset": true</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "tuningConfig": {</span> </span><span><span>            "type": "kafka"</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "dataSchema": {</span> </span><span><span>            "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>            "timestampSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "column": "__time",</span> </span><span><span>                "format": "iso"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "dimensionsSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "dimensions": [</span> </span><span><span>                    "username",</span> </span><span><span>                    "post_title",</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "views"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "upvotes"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "comments"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    "edited"</span> </span><span><span>                ]</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "granularitySpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "queryGranularity": "none",</span> </span><span><span>                "rollup": false,</span> </span><span><span>                "segmentGranularity": "hour"</span> </span><span><span>            }</span> </span><span><span>        }</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 1359</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>    "type": "kafka",</span> </span><span><span>    "spec": {</span> </span><span><span>        "ioConfig": {</span> </span><span><span>            "type": "kafka",</span> </span><span><span>            "consumerProperties": {</span> </span><span><span>                "bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9094"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "topic": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>            "inputFormat": {</span> </span><span><span>                "type": "json"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "useEarliestOffset": true</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "tuningConfig": {</span> </span><span><span>            "type": "kafka"</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "dataSchema": {</span> </span><span><span>            "dataSource": "social_media",</span> </span><span><span>            "timestampSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "column": "__time",</span> </span><span><span>                "format": "iso"</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "dimensionsSpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "dimensions": [</span> </span><span><span>                    "username",</span> </span><span><span>                    "post_title",</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "views"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "upvotes"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    {</span> </span><span><span>                        "type": "long",</span> </span><span><span>                        "name": "comments"</span> </span><span><span>                    },</span> </span><span><span>                    "edited"</span> </span><span><span>                ]</span> </span><span><span>            },</span> </span><span><span>            "granularitySpec": {</span> </span><span><span>                "queryGranularity": "none",</span> </span><span><span>                "rollup": false,</span> </span><span><span>                "segmentGranularity": "hour"</span> </span><span><span>            }</span> </span><span><span>        }</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

The following example sets the `skipRestartIfUnmodified` flag to true. With this flag set to true, the Supervisor will only restart if there has been a modification to the SupervisorSpec. If left unset, the flag defaults to false.

```shell
curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor?skipRestartIfUnmodified=true" \ 
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ 
--data '{ 
    "type": "kafka", 
    "spec": { 
        "ioConfig": { 
            "type": "kafka", 
            "consumerProperties": { 
                "bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9094" 
            }, 
            "topic": "social_media", 
            "inputFormat": { 
                "type": "json" 
            }, 
            "useEarliestOffset": true 
        }, 
        "tuningConfig": { 
            "type": "kafka" 
        }, 
        "dataSchema": { 
            "dataSource": "social_media", 
            "timestampSpec": { 
                "column": "__time", 
                "format": "iso" 
            }, 
            "dimensionsSpec": { 
                "dimensions": [ 
                    "username", 
                    "post_title", 
                    { 
                        "type": "long", 
                        "name": "views" 
                    }, 
                    { 
                        "type": "long", 
                        "name": "upvotes" 
                    }, 
                    { 
                        "type": "long", 
                        "name": "comments" 
                    }, 
                    "edited" 
                ] 
            }, 
            "granularitySpec": { 
                "queryGranularity": "none", 
                "rollup": false, 
                "segmentGranularity": "hour" 
            } 
        } 
    } 
}' 
```

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "id": "social_media"
> }
> ```

Suspends a single running supervisor. Returns the updated supervisor spec, where the `suspended` property is set to `true`. The suspended supervisor continues to emit logs and metrics.
Indexing tasks remain suspended until you [resume the supervisor](#api-reference-supervisor-api--resume-a-supervisor).

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/suspend`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully shut down supervisor</em></p></div><div><p><em>Supervisor already suspended</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to suspend a running supervisor with the name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/suspend"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/suspend HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

Suspends all supervisors. Note that this endpoint returns an HTTP `200 Success` code message even if there are no supervisors or running supervisors to suspend.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/suspendAll`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully suspended all supervisors</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/suspendAll"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/suspendAll HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "status": "success"
> }
> ```

Resumes indexing tasks for a supervisor. Returns an updated supervisor spec with the `suspended` property set to `false`.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/resume`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully resumed supervisor</em></p></div><div><p><em>Supervisor already running</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example resumes a previously suspended supervisor with name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/resume"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/resume HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**

>   "type": "kafka",
>   "spec": {
>       "dataSchema": {
>           "dataSource": "social_media",
>           "timestampSpec": {
>               "column": "__time",
>               "format": "iso",
>               "missingValue": null
>           },
>           "dimensionsSpec": {

>           "lateMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>           "earlyMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>           "lateMessageRejectionStartDateTime": null,
>           "configOverrides": null,

>       },

>               "type": "onheap",
>               "preserveExistingMetrics": false
>           },

>           "bootstrap.servers": "localhost:9094"
>       },

>       "pollTimeout": 100,
>       "startDelay": "PT5S",
>       "period": "PT30S",
>       "useEarliestOffset": true,
>       "completionTimeout": "PT1800S",
>       "lateMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>       "earlyMessageRejectionPeriod": null,
>       "lateMessageRejectionStartDateTime": null,
>       "configOverrides": null,

>       "stream": "social_media",

Resumes all supervisors. Note that this endpoint returns an HTTP `200 Success` code even if there are no supervisors or suspended supervisors to resume.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/resumeAll`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully resumed all supervisors</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/resumeAll"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/resumeAll HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "status": "success"
> }
> ```

The supervisor must be running for this endpoint to be available.

Resets the specified supervisor. This endpoint clears supervisor metadata, prompting the supervisor to resume data reading. The supervisor restarts from the earliest or latest available position, depending on the value of the `useEarliestOffset` property.
After clearing all stored offsets, the supervisor kills and recreates active tasks, so that tasks begin reading from valid positions.

Use this endpoint to recover from a stopped state due to missing offsets. Use this endpoint with caution as it may result in skipped messages and lead to data loss or duplicate data.

The indexing service keeps track of the latest persisted offsets to provide exactly-once ingestion guarantees across tasks. Subsequent tasks must start reading from where the previous task completed for Druid to accept the generated segments. If the messages at the expected starting offsets are no longer available, the supervisor refuses to start and in-flight tasks fail. Possible causes for missing messages include the message retention period elapsing or the topic being removed and re-created. Use the `reset` endpoint to recover from this condition.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/reset`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully reset supervisor</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example shows how to reset a supervisor with the name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/reset"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/reset HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "id": "social_media"
> }
> ```

The supervisor must be running for this endpoint to be available.

Resets the specified offsets for partitions without resetting the entire set.

This endpoint clears only the stored offsets, prompting the supervisor to resume reading data from the specified offsets.
If there are no stored offsets, the specified offsets are set in the metadata store.

After resetting stored offsets, the supervisor kills and recreates any active tasks pertaining to the specified partitions, so that tasks begin reading specified offsets. For partitions that are not specified in this operation, the supervisor resumes from the last stored offset.

Use this endpoint with caution. It can cause skipped messages, leading to data loss or duplicate data.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/resetOffsets`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully reset offsets</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID</em></p></div></div></div>

---

This section presents the structure and details of the reset offsets metadata payload.

| Field | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | The type of reset offsets metadata payload. It must match the supervisor's `type`. Possible values: `kafka` or `kinesis`. | Yes |
| `partitions` | Object | An object representing the reset metadata. See below for details. | Yes |

The following table defines the fields within the `partitions` object in the reset offsets metadata payload.

| Field | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | Must be set as `end`. Indicates the end sequence numbers for the reset offsets. | Yes |
| `stream` | String | The stream to be reset. It must be a valid stream consumed by the supervisor. | Yes |
| `partitionOffsetMap` | Object | A map of partitions to corresponding offsets for the stream to be reset. | Yes |

The following example shows how to reset offsets for a Kafka supervisor with the name `social_media`. For example, the supervisor is reading from a Kafka topic `ads_media_stream` and has the stored offsets: `{"0": 0, "1": 10, "2": 20, "3": 40}`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/resetOffsets"</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json'</span> </span><span><span>--data-raw '{"type":"kafka","partitions":{"type":"end","stream":"ads_media_stream","partitionOffsetMap":{"0":100, "2": 650}}}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/resetOffsets HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>  "type": "kafka",</span> </span><span><span>  "partitions": {</span> </span><span><span>    "type": "end",</span> </span><span><span>    "stream": "ads_media_stream",</span> </span><span><span>    "partitionOffsetMap": {</span> </span><span><span>      "0": 100,</span> </span><span><span>      "2": 650</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>  }</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div><p>The example operation resets offsets only for partitions <code>0</code> and <code>2</code> to 100 and 650 respectively. After a successful reset, when the supervisor's tasks restart, they resume reading from <code>{"0": 100, "1": 10, "2": 650, "3": 40}</code>.</p></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "id": "social_media"
> }
> ```

Terminates a supervisor and its associated indexing tasks, triggering the publishing of their segments. When you terminate a supervisor, Druid places a tombstone marker in the metadata store to prevent reloading on restart.

The terminated supervisor still exists in the metadata store and its history can be retrieved.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/terminate`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully terminated a supervisor</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID or supervisor not running</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/terminate"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/terminate HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "id": "social_media"
> }
> ```

Terminates all supervisors. Terminated supervisors still exist in the metadata store and their history can be retrieved. Note that this endpoint returns an HTTP `200 Success` code even if there are no supervisors or running supervisors to terminate.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/terminateAll`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully terminated all supervisors</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/terminateAll"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/terminateAll HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "status": "success"
> }
> ```

Trigger handoff for specified task groups of a supervisor early. This is a best effort API and makes no guarantees of handoff execution.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/taskGroups/handoff`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>202 ACCEPTED</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Request has been accepted and handoff will be initiated in the background.</em></p></div><div><p><em>Invalid supervisor ID or the supervisor is not running.</em></p></div><div><p><em>Supervisor does not support early handoff.</em></p></div></div></div>

The following example shows how to handoff task groups for a supervisor with the name `social_media` and has the task groups: `1,2,3`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl --request POST "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/taskGroups/handoff"</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json'</span> </span><span><span>--data-raw '{"taskGroupIds": [1, 2, 3]}'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/social_media/taskGroups/handoff HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>{</span> </span><span><span>  "taskGroupIds": [1, 2, 3],</span> </span><span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **202 Accepted**
> *Empty response*

Shuts down a supervisor. This endpoint is deprecated and will be removed in future releases. Use the equivalent [terminate](#api-reference-supervisor-api--terminate-a-supervisor) endpoint instead.

`POST` `/druid/indexer/v1/supervisor/{supervisorId}/shutdown`

---

<a id="api-reference-retention-rules-api"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://druid.apache.org/docs/latest/api-reference/retention-rules-api/ -->

<!-- page_index: 131 -->

# Retention rules API

This topic describes the API endpoints for managing retention rules in Apache Druid. You can configure retention rules in the Druid web console or API.

Druid uses retention rules to determine what data is retained in the cluster. Druid supports load, drop, and broadcast rules. For more information, see [Using rules to drop and retain data](#operations-rule-configuration).

In this topic, `http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT` is a placeholder for your Router service address and port. Replace it with the information for your deployment. For example, use `http://localhost:8888` for quickstart deployments.

Updates one or more retention rules for a datasource. The request body takes an array of retention rule objects. For details on defining retention rules, see the following sources:

- [Load rules](#operations-rule-configuration--load-rules)
- [Drop rules](#operations-rule-configuration--drop-rules)
- [Broadcast rules](#operations-rule-configuration--broadcast-rules)

This request overwrites any existing rules for the datasource.
Druid reads rules in the order in which they appear; for more information, see [rule structure](#operations-rule-configuration).

Note that this endpoint returns an HTTP `200 OK` even if the datasource does not exist.

`POST` `/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/{dataSource}`

The endpoint supports a set of optional header parameters to populate the `author` and `comment` fields in the `auditInfo` property for audit history.

- `X-Druid-Author` (optional)
  - Type: String
  - A string representing the author making the configuration change.
- `X-Druid-Comment` (optional)
  - Type: String
  - A string describing the update.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully updated retention rules for specified datasource</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example sets a set of broadcast, load, and drop retention rules for the `kttm1` datasource.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/kttm1" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'X-Druid-Author: doc intern' \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'X-Druid-Comment: submitted via api' \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '[</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "broadcastForever"</span> </span><span><span>    },</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "loadForever",</span> </span><span><span>        "tieredReplicants": {</span> </span><span><span>            "_default_tier": 2</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "useDefaultTierForNull": true</span> </span><span><span>    },</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "dropByPeriod",</span> </span><span><span>        "period": "P1M"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>]'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/coordinator/v1/rules/kttm1 HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>X-Druid-Author: doc intern</span> </span><span><span>X-Druid-Comment: submitted via api</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 273</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>[</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "broadcastForever"</span> </span><span><span>    },</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "loadForever",</span> </span><span><span>        "tieredReplicants": {</span> </span><span><span>            "_default_tier": 1</span> </span><span><span>        },</span> </span><span><span>        "useDefaultTierForNull": true</span> </span><span><span>    },</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "dropByPeriod",</span> </span><span><span>        "period": "P1M"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

A successful request returns an HTTP `200 OK` message code and an empty response body.

Updates one or more default retention rules for all datasources. Submit retention rules as an array of objects in the request body. For details on defining retention rules, see the following sources:

- [Load rules](#operations-rule-configuration--load-rules)
- [Drop rules](#operations-rule-configuration--drop-rules)
- [Broadcast rules](#operations-rule-configuration--broadcast-rules)

This request overwrites any existing rules for all datasources. To remove default retention rules for all datasources, submit an empty rule array in the request body. Rules are read in the order in which they appear; for more information, see [rule structure](#operations-rule-configuration).

`POST` `/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/_default`

The endpoint supports a set of optional header parameters to populate the `author` and `comment` fields in the `auditInfo` property for audit history.

- `X-Druid-Author` (optional)
  - Type: String
  - A string representing the author making the configuration change.
- `X-Druid-Comment` (optional)
  - Type: String
  - A string describing the update.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>500 SERVER ERROR</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully updated default retention rules</em></p></div><div><p><em>Error with request body</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example updates the default retention rule for all datasources with a `loadByInterval` rule.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/_default" \</span> </span><span><span>--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \</span> </span><span><span>--data '[</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "loadByInterval",</span> </span><span><span>        "tieredReplicants": {},</span> </span><span><span>        "useDefaultTierForNull": false,</span> </span><span><span>        "interval": "2010-01-01/2020-01-01"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>]'</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>POST /druid/coordinator/v1/rules/_default HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span><span><span>Content-Type: application/json</span> </span><span><span>Content-Length: 205</span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span>[</span> </span><span><span>    {</span> </span><span><span>        "type": "loadByInterval",</span> </span><span><span>        "tieredReplicants": {},</span> </span><span><span>        "useDefaultTierForNull": false,</span> </span><span><span>        "interval": "2010-01-01/2020-01-01"</span> </span><span><span>    }</span> </span><span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

A successful request returns an HTTP `200 OK` message code and an empty response body.

Retrieves all current retention rules in the cluster including the default retention rule. Returns an array of objects for each datasource and their associated retention rules.

`GET` `/druid/coordinator/v1/rules`

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved retention rules</em></p></div></div></div>

---

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/coordinator/v1/rules"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/coordinator/v1/rules HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> {
>   "_default": [
>       {
>           "tieredReplicants": {
>               "_default_tier": 2
>           },
>           "type": "loadForever"
>       }
>   ],
>   "social_media": [
>       {
>           "interval": "2023-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2023-02-01T00:00:00.000Z",
>           "type": "dropByInterval"
>       }
>   ],
>   "wikipedia_api": [],
> }
> ```

Retrieves an array of rule objects for a single datasource. Returns an empty array if there are no retention rules.

Note that this endpoint returns an HTTP `200 OK` message code even if the datasource doesn't exist.

`GET` `/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/{dataSource}`

- `full` (optional)
  - Includes the default retention rule for the datasource in the response.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved retention rules</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example retrieves the custom retention rules and default retention rules for datasource with the name `social_media`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/social_media?full=null"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/coordinator/v1/rules/social_media?full=null HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   {
>       "interval": "2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2022-02-01T00:00:00.000Z",
>       "type": "dropByInterval"
>   },
>   {
>       "interval": "2010-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
>       "tieredReplicants": {
>           "_default_tier": 2
>       },
>       "type": "loadByInterval"
>   },
>   {
>       "tieredReplicants": {
>           "_default_tier": 2
>       },
>       "type": "loadForever"
>   }
> ]
> ```

Retrieves the audit history of rules for all datasources over an interval of time. The default interval is 1 week. You can change this period by setting `druid.audit.manager.auditHistoryMillis` in the `runtime.properties` file for the Coordinator.

`GET` `/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/history`

Note that the following query parameters cannot be chained.

- `interval` (optional)
  - Type: ISO 8601.
  - Limits the number of results to the specified time interval. Delimit with `/`. For example, `2023-07-13/2023-07-19`.
- `count` (optional)
  - Type: Int
  - Limits the number of results to the last `n` entries.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>200 SUCCESS</li><li>400 BAD REQUEST</li><li>404 NOT FOUND</li></ul><div><div><p><em>Successfully retrieved audit history</em></p></div><div><p><em>Request in the incorrect format</em></p></div><div><p><em><code>count</code> query parameter too large</em></p></div></div></div>

---

The following example retrieves the audit history for all datasources from `2023-07-13` to `2023-07-19`.

<div class="tabs-container tabList__CuJ"><ul><li>cURL</li><li>HTTP</li></ul><div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>curl "http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT/druid/coordinator/v1/rules/history?interval=2023-07-13%2F2023-07-19"</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><pre><code><span><span>GET /druid/coordinator/v1/rules/history?interval=2023-07-13/2023-07-19 HTTP/1.1</span> </span><span><span>Host: http://ROUTER_IP:ROUTER_PORT</span> </span></code></pre><div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

> [!NOTE]
> **View the response**
> ```json
> [
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[{\"interval\":\"2023-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2023-02-01T00:00:00.000Z\",\"type\":\"dropByInterval\"}]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-13T18:05:33.066Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:10:21.203Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "wikipedia_api",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[{\"tieredReplicants\":{\"_default_tier\":2},\"type\":\"loadForever\"}]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:10:44.519Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "wikipedia_api",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:11:02.110Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[{\"interval\":\"2023-07-03T18:49:54.848Z/2023-07-03T18:49:55.861Z\",\"type\":\"dropByInterval\"}]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:32:50.060Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[{\"interval\":\"2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2022-02-01T00:00:00.000Z\",\"type\":\"dropByInterval\"}]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:34:09.657Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
>           "comment": "test",
>           "ip": "127.0.0.1"
>       },
>       "payload": "[{\"interval\":\"2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z/2022-02-01T00:00:00.000Z\",\"type\":\"dropByInterval\"},{\"tieredReplicants\":{\"_default_tier\":2},\"type\":\"loadForever\"}]",
>       "auditTime": "2023-07-18T18:38:37.223Z"
>   },
>   {
>       "key": "social_media",
>       "type": "rules",
>       "auditInfo": {
>           "author": "console",
